Title: Anwar hails Muslim-Hindu NGO peace effort, says it reflects national maturity
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim welcomed the peace effort by non-governmental organisations representing the Muslim and Hindu communities, saying they reflect the maturity of the people in choosing the path of peace, rejecting division and upholding the spirit of mutual respect.
Anwar said the peace effort embodies the true spirit of Malaysia, a country firmly grounded in the values of kindness, togetherness, and inter-racial respect.
"We must continue to preserve and nurture this unity, as it is the foundation of our nation's strength," he said in a statement today.
The prime minister also lauded all parties involved, including the police, who contributed to the success of the initiative.
"May it continue to unite us and serve as an example for strengthening national harmony."
Yesterday, the NGOs called for all racial issues involving both groups to be resolved promptly through a comprehensive
peace initiative
.
During a dialogue session involving several NGOs, held under the Madani Harmony Programme in Putrajaya yesterday, participants agreed that negotiation must be the pathway to finding common ground in addressing these matters.
Malaysian Muslim Lawyers Association president Datuk Zainul Rijal Abu Bakar said the dialogue provided a platform for both communities to engage directly, free from external influence.
He said they are working to extinguish current hotspots and intend to engage in direct dialogue and discussions with one another, rather than responding to third-party commentary or provocation.
This was seen as a first step, with the aim of moving forward.
Zainul said the first round of negotiations involved about 20 Malay representatives and 10 Indian representatives, all committed to seeking a harmonious solution.
Meanwhile, Malaysian Hindu and Temples Association president Datuk N. Sivakumar said the meeting was crucial in pursuing a peaceful resolution.
The dialogue session, held behind closed doors, lasted four hours from 3pm.
Among those in attendance were Anti-Illegal Houses of Worship Movement coordinator Muhammad Zamri Vinoth, activist Cikgu Chandra (real name S. Chandrasegaran), independent preacher Firdaus Wong, and activist and influencer Kumaresan Jothi, also known as Santhanar Dronachariyar.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Petronas Chemicals shares rally to one-year high on late buying
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Shares of Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd (PetChem) jumped nearly 11 per cent in the final trading hour, as late buying pushed the stock to a more than one-year high.
The counter, which opened 20 sen or 4.08 per cent higher at RM5.10, remained unchanged until the midday break before climbing about an hour after trading resumed, hitting a high of RM5.43.
At the time of writing, PetChem was up 46 sen or 9.39 per cent at RM5.36, a level not seen since November 4, 2024. It was the third most actively traded counter, with 45.29 million shares changing hands, about 5.8 times its 200-day volume moving average.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, PetChem said the Employees Provident Fund (EPF) has increased its stake in the company after acquiring 6.6 million shares in the open market on March 16.
The shares were bought in two transactions comprising 3.6 million shares and another three million shares through Citigroup Nominees (Tempatan) Sdn Bhd.
The stock closed at RM4.58 on that day, down 17 sen or 3.58 per cent from RM4.75 previously.
Following the purchases, EPF's direct shareholding in PetChem rose to 1.004 billion shares, representing a 12.56 per cent stake.
PetChem is majority-owned by Petroliam Nasional Bhd with a 64.35 per cent stake, followed by EPF and Permodalan Nasional Bhd at 5.59 per cent.
The group, one of Malaysia's largest listed chemical producers, is involved in the manufacturing and marketing of petrochemical products and has a market capitalisation of more than RM40 billion.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Samsung Elec plans over US$73bil investment in 2026 to lead in AI chip sector
Article:
SEOUL: Samsung Electronics said on Thursday that it plans to invest more than 110 trillion won (US$73.24 billion) this year in research and development and facilitiesin a bid to lead the semiconductor industry in artificial intelligence.
The South Korean company also said in a corporate filing that it was pursuing meaningful mergers and acquisitions in areas including robots, medical technology, auto electronics and air-conditioning solutions.
Last year, Samsung, which is the world's biggest memory chip producer, said it invested a total of 90.4 trillion won, including 52.7 trillion won in capital expenditure and 37.7 trillion won in research and development.
Samsung added that it plans to pay 9.8 trillion won in regular dividends for 2026.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Couple killed in head-on crash after car enters opposite lane
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: A couple was killed in a two-car crash at Km 77.3 of the Johor Baru–Mersing road after one of the vehicles entered the opposite lane.
Kota Tinggi police chief Superintendent Yusof Othman said the victims were travelling in a Nissan Sentra driven by a 26-year-old man.
He said the car veered into the opposite lane before colliding with a Proton Saga carrying four siblings.
"We were alerted to the crash at 9.45am. The Nissan Sentra was carrying a couple, including a 25-year-old woman.
"The Proton Saga was driven by a 23-year-old woman, with three male passengers aged between 16 and 21.
"The Nissan Sentra was travelling from Mersing to Johor Baru, while the Proton Saga was travelling from Pasir Gudang to Kuantan, Pahang," he said in a statement today.
He said the driver and front passenger of the Nissan Sentra suffered severe head and body injuries and were pronounced dead at the scene.
The bodies were taken to the Forensic Department of Kota Tinggi Hospital.
"The occupants of the Proton Saga were also taken to the hospital for treatment," he said.
Yusof said the case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act.
He also advised road users to remain alert and comply with traffic laws to ensure safety.
The deceased woman was identified as P Priyanka, while details of her partner have yet to be obtained.
The Proton Saga occupants, who are siblings, sustained minor injuries.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Kara Satukan Rasa' brings Ramadan joy to Rumah Titian Kaseh
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Kara Malaysia brought the spirit of togetherness this Ramadan through its 'Kara Satukan Rasa' campaign, hosting a meaningful celebration with 50 orphans and 10 single mothers at Rumah Titian Kaseh.
The initiative reflected the many ways Malaysians experience the festive season, from preparing traditional dishes to enjoying simpler moments with loved ones.
(From left) Rumah Titian Kaseh representative Fadilah Muhammad Shaari, Kara Marketing Malaysia director Susanto and UtusanTV chief editor Metra Syahril Mohamed pouring Kara Coconut Cream into the bubur lambuk pot during the Kara Satukan Rasa Bubur Lambuk Distribution Ceremony at Rumah Titian Kaseh, Titiwangsa. - File pic credit (Kara Malaysia)
As part of the programme, Kara team members came together to cook bubur lambuk, a Ramadan staple often prepared in large quantities and shared with the community. Using Kara Coconut Cream as the key ingredient, everyone worked side by side to prepare the dish before distributing it to a nearby mosque, public transport stations and residents.
Celebrity chef Ismail was also present during the cooking session, guiding participants and adding a touch of authenticity to the preparation of the traditional dish.
The activity embodied the message of 'Kara Satukan Rasa', highlighting how shared meals can bring people closer during the holy month.
The gathering later continued into an evening programme featuring performances from Iman Troye, Datin Alyah and Izzue Islam, and the distribution of duit raya to the children and single mothers.
Iman Troye also performed the official
Kara Satukan Rasa Raya
song, adding a festive atmosphere to the gathering and delighting the children and residents of Rumah Titian Kaseh.
Kara Marketing Malaysia director Susanto said the brand has long been part of Malaysian households and festive traditions.
"Since 1999, Kara has accompanied Malaysian families through the flavours and moments that shape our culinary heritage. For us, rasa is more than taste — it reflects familiarity and trust that have been passed down through generations," he said.
For many Malaysians, Kara remains closely associated with santan, often recalled with 'Ingat Santan, Ingat Kara', reflecting the brand's presence in local kitchens.
Kara brand manager Shah Rizal said the 'Kara Satukan Rasa' campaign reflects the many ways Malaysians experience the festive season.
"From preparing juadah during Ramadan to celebrating Raya, and even the simple lepak moments in between, each phase carries its own rasa. Through this campaign, we celebrate how Kara connects traditions, homes and generations," he said.
(From left) Kara Marketing Malaysia director Susanto presents a mock cheque representing an RM3,000 contribution to Rumah Titian Kaseh Manager, Akmal Yusof, during the Kara Satukan Rasa Bubur Lambuk Distribution Ceremony at Rumah Titian Kaseh, Titiwangsa. - File pic credit (Kara Malaysia)
In conjunction with the celebration, Kara Malaysia also contributed RM3,000 in cash and a three-month supply of coconut cream to Rumah Titian Kaseh. This helps them sustain everyday cooking needs beyond Ramadan.
Kara also extended its support to the children of Rumah Titian Kaseh through the contribution of school stationery, aimed at helping ease their educational needs and encouraging them to pursue their studies with confidence. This initiative reflects Kara's continued commitment to giving back to the community and supporting the well-being and future of underprivileged children.
Additional contributions were also provided by UtusanTV, which donated RM2,000 in cash, as well as Akasa, donating cooking ingredients to support the home's kitchen as Raya approached. Dua Kelinci also brought smiles to the children by providing simple treats from Deka and TicTac.
Kara Malaysia expressed its deepest appreciation to Tengku Ampuan of Pahang Tunku Azizah Aminah Maimunah Iskandariah, in enlivening the arrival of Ramadan through the Rewang Welcoming Ramadan programme.
Kara was deeply honoured by Tunku Azizah's gracious choice to include Kara Coconut Cream as the main ingredient in the preparation of Bubur Lambuk Pahang across seven locations during the holy month.
The brand is sincerely grateful for the opportunity to be part of these meaningful efforts and hopes that this noble tradition continues to bring blessings and strengthen the spirit of togetherness within the community.
Also served as part of the special dishes prepared for the children and residents of Rumah Titian Kaseh were the Kara Satukan Rasa special recipes. (From left) Kara Lapis Matcha-Beri, Kara Rendang Gratin, Mangga Cotta and Kara Gula Melaka. - File pic credit (Kara Malaysia)
Through these shared moments, Kara remained close to the people and traditions that define Ramadan and Raya. In keeping with its belief that rasa is not only tasted but felt, 'Kara Satukan Rasa' continues to reflect the role Kara plays in everyday Malaysians lives where food becomes a bridge for care, familiarity and togetherness.
For more information, visit Kara Malaysia's official social media pages on
Facebook
,
Instagram
, and
TikTok
.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bad weather expected to put damper on Raya 'balik kampung' trips
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Heavy rain and thunderstorms are expected to hit several states this evening, putting a damper on Hari Raya Aidilfitri "balik kampung" journeys.
The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) issued a thunderstorm warning at 3.50pm today, forecasting stormy weather until 6pm.
The department said the warning involves Kedah, Perak, Pahang, Selangor, Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya, as well as parts of Sarawak and Sabah.
In Kedah, the affected areas are Kulim and Bandar Baharu, while in Perak, the districts involved are Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang and Muallim.
In Pahang, the warning covers Cameron Highlands, Lipis and Raub.
In Selangor, the affected areas are Hulu Selangor, Gombak, Petaling, Hulu Langat and Sepang. Also affected are Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya.
In Sarawak, the warning involves Sri Aman, Betong, Sibu, Mukah, Bintulu, Miri and Limbang, while in Sabah it affects the interior areas of Sipitang, Tenom and Nabawan, as well as Tawau.
The public can obtain the latest updates via MetMalaysia's official website or the myCuaca mobile application.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Online scams cost Kota Kinabalu victims RM11.1m in nearly 3 months
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: Online fraud cases in Kota Kinabalu caused losses of RM11.12 million from 214 cases recorded between January and March 15, police said.
Acting district police chief Superintendent Syed Lot Syed Ab Rahman said the cases formed part of 290 commercial crime investigations opened by the Commercial Crime Investigation Division, with total losses amounting to RM14.628 million.
He said 46 cases involved e-commerce fraud, particularly vehicle transactions, resulting in losses of RM328,888.
Explaining the tactic, Syed Lot said syndicates acted as intermediaries by reposting images of vehicles owned by others on platforms such as Facebook and Mudah.my at significantly lower prices.
"When victims show interest, the syndicates negotiate the price and direct them to the original owner's address to view the vehicle. At the same time, they inform the real owner that a representative will be coming to inspect the car.
"Once the victim agrees to buy, the syndicates request payment before supposedly handing over the vehicle," he said in a statement on Thursday.
He said victims who transferred money to the syndicate's account were then left without the vehicle, as the real owners had not received any payment, while the scammers cut off all contact.
Investigations found the syndicates operated purely as third-party intermediaries online and never met either the victims or the genuine owners.
Syed Lot also warned of scams involving fake homestay bookings promoted on social media, particularly in urban and tourist areas during school holidays and festive seasons.
He urged the public to remain vigilant and to verify the identity of vehicle owners or service providers before making any payments.
"Do not be easily swayed by advertisements or offers that seem too cheap," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US expands visa bond rule to 12 more countries
Article:
WASHINGTON: The United States (US) said on Wednesday it is expanding its visa bond programme to 12 additional countries, reported German Press Agency (dpa).
From April 2, travellers from a total of 50 countries will be required to pay a deposit of US$15,000 upon entry to the US, which the government says is intended to prevent visitors from overstaying their visas.
Tunisia, a participant in this year's World Cup, has been added to the list, as have Cambodia, Ethiopia, Georgia, Grenada, Lesotho, Mauritius, Mongolia, Mozambique, Nicaragua, Papua New Guinea and the Seychelles.
The pilot scheme was launched last August and applies to tourists and business travellers.
The State Department said the number of visa holders overstaying their permitted stay has fallen significantly since.
The deposit is to be returned to travellers who return home under the terms of their visa or do not proceed with their travel plans.
Visitors from countries in the so-called Visa Waiver Programme (VWP) are not affected, according to earlier information. This includes most EU countries.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UK wages rise at slowest pace in over five years
Article:
LONDON: British wages, excluding bonuses, rose at their slowest pace since late 2020 in the three months to January, according to official data published on Thursday which also suggested a weakening in employment might have bottomed out.
Regular earnings rose by 3.8 per cent in the November-to-January period, the slowest increase since the three months to November 2020 and down from 4.1 per cent in the final quarter of 2025, the Office for National Statistics said.
Economists polled by Reuters had mostly expected pay growth of 4.0 per cent.
The ONS data also showed Britain's unemployment rate - which is calculated from a survey that the ONS is still overhauling  - held at 5.2 per cent, its highest since the COVID-19 pandemic period.
The number of people in payrolled employment rose by a provisional estimate of 20,000 people between February and January.
In January, payrolls rose by a revised estimate of 6,000 compared with a provisional estimate of a fall of 11,000.
Until this month, the Bank of England had been trying to gauge whether lingering inflation heat in the labour market or a weakening of hiring in recent months posed the bigger risk to the economy.
But new inflation pressures have emerged, caused by the jump in energy prices after the start of the war in the Middle East.
The BoE is expected to keep borrowing costs on hold at 1200 GMT on Thursday at the end of the Monetary Policy Committee's March meeting which until recently had been expected to result in a quarter-point rate cut.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Envacgen becomes Vietnam's first EV-A71 vaccine, paving way for Southeast Asia expansion
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Medigen Vaccine Biologics Corp (MVC) and French pharmaceutical company Substipharm Biologics announced that their enterovirus 71 (EV-A71) vaccine, Envacgen, has received marketing authorisation from the Drug Administration of Vietnam (DAV).
The launch of Envacgen in Vietnam not only addresses a critical public health need but also underscores the potential for innovative vaccine solutions to reshape paediatric care across Southeast Asia.
In Malaysia, EV-A71 has been a recurring public health concern, particularly in young children under five. The country has experienced cyclical outbreaks of hand, foot, and mouth disease (HFMD), with EV-A71 being one of the most virulent strains.
However, as of now, there is no locally approved EV-A71 vaccine available in Malaysia. This makes regional approvals like Vietnam's Envacgen an important milestone for potential Southeast Asia rollout, including Malaysia.
The Vietnam approval is expected to serve as a springboard for wider regional expansion as demand grows for more effective HFMD prevention measures.
Substipharm Biologics will lead the marketing and distribution of Envacgen in Vietnam, marking a historic milestone as the first EV-A71 vaccine approved in the country. The move builds on Envacgen's dominant 97 per cent market share in Taiwan and positions both companies to expand their footprint across Southeast Asia.
Leo Lee, chief executive officer (CEO) of MVC, said the authorisation is a key milestone in the company's global growth strategy.
"We are eager to work with our experienced partner, Substipharm Biologics, to provide this high-quality vaccine to local children. Facing the vast demand in Southeast Asia, MVC has already initiated capacity expansion plans to ensure a stable supply of premium vaccines," Leo said.
EV-A71 is a leading cause of severe neurological complications and mortality among young children in Asia. Vietnam has been hit repeatedly by outbreaks, recording about 180,000 cases and 31 deaths in 2023 alone, placing a heavy burden on the local healthcare system.
Envacgen offers 100 per cent efficacy, early protection for infants from two months of age, and durable immunity lasting over five years. With Vietnam registering about 1.36 million births annually, the partnership combines MVC's high-quality manufacturing with Substipharm Biologics' marketing expertise to address significant unmet paediatric needs in the region.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cabinet ministers extend Ugadi greetings, highlight importance of unity
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Several cabinet ministers have extended their Ugadi greetings today, urging the nation's diverse communities to continue reinforcing unity and harmony.
Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said the Ugadi festival marked not only the start of a new year, but also a period of hope, renewal, and the beginning of a fresher, more meaningful chapter in life.
"Let us all cherish the values of our inherited traditions while continuing to strengthen unity and harmony in our country's diverse society. May this Ugadi celebration bring lasting well-being, prosperity and happiness to all," he said in a Facebook post today.
Also extending her greetings was Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh, who wished a joyful Ugadi and a happy new year to all her Telugu friends.
Ugadi marks the beginning of the new year in the Hindu lunisolar calendar and is celebrated with great joy by the community observing the festival.
The word Ugadi is derived from the Sanskrit words Yuga, meaning age, and Adi, meaning beginning. It is particularly celebrated by communities in South India, including those in Andhra Pradesh, Telangana and Karnataka.
In Malaysia, around 500,000 members of the Telugu community celebrated Ugadi as the start of a new year, a time that brings hope, renewal and blessings.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 25,000 chickens on sale at RM5 each in Masjid Tanah constituency
Article:
ALOR GAJAH: A total of 25,000 fresh chickens were sold at RM5 each at Masjid Tanah parliamentary constituency today in conjunction with the celebration of three years of the administration of Melaka Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh on March 31.
In a statement, the Media and Communications Unit of the Melaka Chief Minister's Office announced that the initiative is also aimed at helping the people, especially residents of Masjid Tanah constituency, prepare for Aidilfitri, as well as to ease the cost of living.
The statement said the chicken sales programme was held at the offices of the State Legislative Assembly Development and Coordination Committee (Japerun) of the five state constituencies under the Masjid Tanah parliamentary constituency, namely Tanjung Bidara, Ayer Limau, Taboh Naning, Kuala Linggi and Lendu, starting from 8 am to 12 noon today.
"Each state constituency has prepared a sale of 5,000 chickens and it received an encouraging response from the public who came to get chicken supplies at special prices.
"To ensure fair distribution, each individual is limited to purchasing six chickens only," the statement said.
In addition, the Rahmah Madani Sales Programme was also held at the Tanjung Bidara Japerun Office to give the people the opportunity to shop for various items for Hari Raya Aidilfitri preparations at cheaper prices.
In the same statement, Ab Rauf said the initiative was not just a sale but an ongoing effort by the state government to ensure that the people were not burdened when preparing for Aidilfitri.
He said the welfare of the people would continue to be the main focus of his administration in line with the Melaka Sayang Rakyat concept which emphasises caring for the well-being of the people.
He said such efforts would continue to be strengthened from time to time to ensure that the benefits could be felt by more people of Melaka.
The statement added that in conjunction with the sales programme, other state constituencies, namely Pengkalan Batu, Paya Rumput, Telok Mas, Asahan and Pantai Kundor, were also offered chicken and meat sales at Rahmah prices.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PAAB establishes sustainable Islamic finance framework with blue finance elements
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Pengurusan Aset Air Bhd (PAAB) has established Malaysia's first sustainable Islamic finance framework that incorporates blue finance elements, with Maybank Investment Bank Bhd (Maybank IB) serving as the sole sustainability structuring adviser.
PABB is a wholly owned company of the Minister of Finance (Incorporated) and plays a central role in the government's efforts, under the Ministry of Energy Transition and Water Transformation (PETRA), to restructure Malaysia's water services industry towards greater efficiency, improved service quality, and long-term sustainability.
In a joint statement today, PAAB and Maybank IB said the framework marks a significant milestone as the first in Malaysia to incorporate blue finance elements within an Islamic finance structure.
"The framework allows PAAB to undertake blue instruments such as Blue Sukuk and other Islamic financings in adherence with the Guidelines for Blue Finance Version 2.0 developed by the International Finance Corporation in 2025, as well as the Bonds to Finance the Sustainable Blue Economy - A Practitioner's Guide published by the International Capital Market Association in 2023.
"PAAB's blue instruments will support its role in strengthening the efficiency, resilience and sustainability of Malaysia's water sector, while contributing to the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals," they said.
The statement said RAM Sustainability has independently reviewed the framework and accorded it a "platinum" sustainable finance rating under its second-party opinion (SPO), confirming alignment with relevant local, regional, and international sustainable finance standards.
"PAAB is the first organisation to achieve the Platinum distinction, the highest tier under RAM Sustainability's SPO assessment, underscoring the robustness of its sustainability governance and providing strong assurance to investors in its capital-raising activities under the framework," it said.
Meanwhile, PAAB chief executive officer (CEO) Zulkiflee Omar said the framework reinforces PAAB's role as Malaysia's national water asset custodian and a catalyst for water sector transformation.
"By embedding sustainable financing into our strategy, we are aligning with the national agenda by ensuring every asset/project funded strengthens our resilience, efficiency, and sustainability as well as ensuring water security for future generations," he said.
Furthermore, Maybank IB CEO Michael Oh-Lau said the collaboration reflects Maybank's commitment under its ROAR30 strategy to mobilise RM300 billion in sustainable finance by 2030, alongside a target of RM100 billion in new economy financing to support sectors driving a low-carbon, inclusive and future-ready economy.
He said the collaboration builds on RM176.12 billion in sustainable finance mobilised between 2021 and 2025.
"With the blue economy emerging as a national priority, we are honoured to partner with PAAB in structuring Malaysia's first Sustainable Islamic Finance Framework with integrated blue finance features.
"This initiative addresses the urgent need for sustainable capital in water infrastructure while supporting the nation's efforts to reduce non-revenue water," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Yinson Q4 profit falls 64.5pct on absence of tax credit, lower EPCIC contributions
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Yinson Holdings Bhd's net profit for the fourth quarter ended Jan 31, 2026 (Q4 FY26), fell 64.5 per cent to RM228 million from RM643 million a year ago.
In a filing to Bursa Malaysia, the group said the decline reflected the absence of a RM704 million income tax credit recognised in Q4 FY25 following a change in tax basis for Yinson's Offshore Production operations in the Netherlands.
This was partially offset by higher contributions from joint ventures and associates during the quarter.
Meanwhile, quarterly revenue dropped 20 per cent to RM1.12 billion from RM1.4 billion, mainly due to lower contributions from engineering, procurement, construction, installation, and commissioning (EPCIC) activities.
Yinson said the fall was partially mitigated by stronger operational earnings from these assets and a RM340 million gain recognised in Q3 FY26 from the buyout of the project loan related to the floating production, storage, and offloading unit (FPSO) Atlanta.
Earnings per share (EPS) fell 76.6 per cent to 4.70 sen from 20.10 sen a year ago.
For the full financial year, Yinson's net profit declined 45.4 per cent to RM683 million from RM1.25 billion, while revenue slipped 28.5 per cent to RM5.44 billion from RM7.61 billion previously.
The board declared a final single-tier dividend of 1.0 sen per share, totalling RM29 million, payable on June 18, 2026. This brings the total dividend for FY26 to five sen per share, up 22 per cent from FY25, highlighting the group's ability to return capital to shareholders as its assets become operational.
Yinson Group executive chairman Lim Han Weng said the steady financial performance continues to be underpinned by the steady contribution of all its business units.
"Yinson Renewables' acquisition of the Mt Cass Wind Farm project in New Zealand marks the first wind farm in its portfolio under construction and strengthens the value of our development pipeline.
"The 15-year power purchase agreement and exclusivity agreement signed with Genesis Energy further reinforces our position as a trusted partner in the country's energy transition," he said in a separate statement.
On prospects, Yinson said it continues to implement prudent risk management measures amidst ongoing macroeconomic headwinds, including oil price and foreign exchange volatility, rising financing costs, and rapidly evolving geopolitics.
These measures include hedging, inflation-linked contract structures, cost diversification, and operational efficiency initiatives.
Yinson remains confident in its resilience and is optimistic about delivering satisfactory results for the financial year ending 31 January 2027. This is backed by a robust portfolio of long-term contracted revenues, a fully operational fleet, and a diversified platform across offshore production, renewables, and green technologies.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gas producer Energean halts Israel 2026 outlook as Mideast war shuts production
Article:
Reuters
LONDON: Eastern Mediterranean-focused gas producer Energean on Thursday suspended its 2026 outlook for Israel after the Middle East conflict forced the shutdown of its production vessel that serves multiple Israeli fields.
Growing regional tensions triggered precautionary shutdowns of key oil and gas facilities across the Middle East, including Qatar's LNG operations, Israeli offshore fields, and production sites in Iraqi Kurdistan.
The company said it will assess the impact on its 2026 production forecast once the duration and full effect of the shutdown are clear.
Energean also reported an annual adjusted core profit of US$1.12 billion, down from US$1.16 billion a year earlier. It had previously expected fiscal 2026 production to be in the range of 140-150 thousand barrels of oil equivalent per day.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Biscuit maker sleeps three hours a day to meet Raya demand
Article:
KEMAMAN: A biscuit entrepreneur in Kampung Gong Pauh is sleeping just three hours a day as she works to fulfil more than 6,000 orders for festive treats ahead of Aidilfitri.
Aminah Akmarina Osman, 40, said she and six workers have extended their operations until 3am to cope with increased demand.
She said orders had risen significantly compared with last year, when she produced about 4,000 jars.
"Due to the increase in orders, we have to continue baking until early morning. Normally, we work from 8am to 5pm," she said.
Aminah offers 16 types of biscuits, including Sarang Semut, Bunga Cina Klasik, Cornflakes Crunchy, Choco Almond, Red Velvet Bueno, Lotus Biscoff, pineapple tarts and chocolate chip cookies.
Among the most popular are Kunafa Pistachio, Dark Salted Caramel, Callebaut cookies and Black Sea Salt.
She said orders are not prepared too early to ensure freshness.
"If they are made too early, the taste may not be as good as freshly baked biscuits," she said.
In addition to local customers, she receives orders from Kuala Lumpur, Pahang, Johor and Selangor.
Aminah said she caters to different preferences, from traditional recipes using simpler ingredients to premium versions made with higher-quality materials.
The biscuits are priced between RM23 and RM45 per jar.
A former employee of a state government-linked company, she left her job in 2020 to care for her late mother.
"I had no experience in cooking or baking. After I resigned, I learned everything online and carried out my own research and development to create recipes that suit customers' tastes," she said.
She added that biscuit production is focused during Ramadan, while on regular days she prepares other dishes, including cakes, nasi Arab, nasi ayam and noodles for customers.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Thailand's new parliament elects Anutin as PM
Article:
BANGKOK: Thailand's new parliament on Thursday elected Anutin Charnvirakul as prime minister, keeping the conservative in the top office after his party routed its election rivals.
"This parliament has voted for Anutin Charnvirakul to become prime minister," House Speaker Sophon Zaram said on the floor of the legislature, noting the incumbent had garnered more than half of the ballots cast.
Anutin received 293 votes from newly seated lawmakers to win the premiership, with his progressive rival Natthaphong Ruengpanyawut gaining 119 and 86 MPs abstaining, Sophon said.
"I hope to remain in my position to serve the people for as long as I can," Anutin told reporters ahead of the vote.
"Those who know me understand that whenever there is a problem affecting the public, I will respond immediately to their needs."
The Southeast Asian nation's new government will have to handle the fallout from the Middle East war, sluggish economic growth and lingering border tensions with neighbor Cambodia.
Anutin's anointment comes after his pro-military and pro-monarchy Bhumjaithai party had its best electoral performance ever in February, following two rounds of deadly border clashes with Cambodia last year.
Bhumjaithai promised to build a wall on the Cambodian frontier, keep all border crossings closed and recruit 100,000 volunteer soldiers, winning the most seats of any party and putting Anutin in pole position to head the next government.
The third-placed Pheu Thai party of jailed former prime minister Thaksin Shinawatra agreed to join Anutin in a coalition alongside 14 smaller parties, and parliament anointed him on Thursday.
The 59-year-old millionaire heir to a family construction fortune – who championed the decriminalisation of cannabis in Thailand – was first elected prime minister in September.
He came to office after his predecessor, Paetongtarn Shinawatra, Thaksin's daughter, was ousted by court order over an ethics complaint.
In a leaked phone conversation, Paetongtarn referred to former Cambodian leader Hun Sun as "uncle" and called a Thai military commander her "opponent", triggering public and political outrage.
[[nid:1400335]]
The Pheu Thai-led government fell after Anutin pulled Bhumjaithai out of the then coalition, and parliament later elected him prime minister.
The reformist People's Party – which had been polling first ahead of the election but ultimately came in second – will lead the opposition.
But 10 of its newly elected MPs, including party leader Natthaphong, face accusations of an ethics breach over their effort to reform the royal insult law, which could see them banned from politics.
Speaking to journalists before the vote, Natthaphong said the opposition aimed to "utilise this parliamentary stage to effectively communicate with our fellow citizens."
Challenges ahead
Thailand's economic growth slowed last year, with the incoming government facing a struggling tourism sector while fast-growing Vietnam is now attracting more foreign direct investment.
The day after Israeli-US strikes on Iran, which ignited the war that has thrown global markets into turmoil, Anutin pledged to "turn this Middle East crisis into an opportunity for Thailand."
But the country is now reckoning with higher fuel prices and supply disruptions.
Political scientist Yuttaporn Issarachai said that the Thai government's primary agenda has shifted towards daunting external challenges, including the economic fallout of the Middle East conflagration.
"So far, the cabinet has failed to produce decisive measures to lower fuel costs, offering only 'band-aid' solutions like working from home," he told AFP.
The longstanding border conflict with Cambodia remains a challenge – even while a fragile ceasefire remains in place.
Soon after becoming premier the first time, Anutin authorised the armed forces to take whatever action they saw fit on the frontier.
The countries' 800-kilometre (500-mile) boundary is still not fully agreed, and Thailand's military took control of several disputed areas in the latest fighting in December.
But voters' attention had moved elsewhere, Yuttaporn said.
"The economy is now the public's top priority."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Retail reimagined: CityWalk and rise of AI-driven consumer spaces
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Retail Group Malaysia (RGM) has maintained its projection of 4.0 per cent growth in retail sales for 2026, although it cautioned that the outlook does not factor in potential cost-of-living pressures arising from the ongoing conflict in the Middle East.
The forecast aligns with the government's expectation for Malaysia's economy to expand between 4.0 per cent and 4.5 per cent this year, supported by resilient household spending, sustained private and public investments, stronger exports, and a rise in tourist arrivals.
With 2026 designated as Visit Malaysia Year, the government is targeting 47 million international tourist arrivals and RM329 billion in tourism receipts. This is expected to drive higher spending from both domestic and foreign tourists.
RGM said the anticipated surge in tourism-related spending will benefit retail businesses, particularly those located in major cities and key tourism destinations across the country.
Jason Tan, an expert in repositioning and curating retail assets across the Klang Valley, said retail performance is driven by how well the concept aligns with the needs of the catchment.
Drawing from projects such as 163 Retail Park, The Campus Ampang, and Central i-City Mall, he said that curated, experience-led retail formats can outperform larger, generic malls.
"A retail destination does not need to be massive to establish itself. What matters is whether the concept is coherent, whether the adjacencies make sense and whether the place gives people a clear reason to visit.
"In fact, a more compact format can be an advantage because it allows you to curate more deliberately, create a stronger sense of identity and avoid becoming too diluted," he said.
This thinking underpins the development of CityWalk, a new retail precinct at i-City linked to Central i-City Mall via an overhead walkway. At about 200,000 sq ft, CityWalk is designed to support a more focused, experience-led concept rather than function as a conventional retail extension.
As master developer, I-Berhad has progressively transformed i-City into a technology-integrated township combining business, hospitality, tourism and digital infrastructure. Anchors such as Mercu Maybank, Central i-City Mall, international hotels and a Tier-3 data centre form the foundation of a broader AI-driven ecosystem.
In this context, CityWalk functions as the marketplace layer of i-City's AI ecosystem - translating AI and robotics from infrastructure into everyday consumer use cases.
Central i-City Mall itself has emerged as the township's retail anchor, with occupancy reaching about 95 per cent, demonstrating the location's ability to sustain strong retail demand. This provides CityWalk with an immediate advantage, as it benefits from existing footfall, tenant confidence and destination familiarity.
The overhead walkway creates continuity between the mall and CityWalk, allowing both to function as part of a single, integrated destination," Tan said, adding that this connectivity could generate positive spillover effects for the wider development.
He noted that i-City is also evolving beyond its original identity as a digital city. Initiatives such as the AI World Experience Centre (AIWEC) and AI Living are embedding artificial intelligence, robotics and smart living into everyday experiences.
"This evolution positions i-City not just as a place to live and work but as a platform where AI, robotics and smart living are increasingly embedded into everyday experiences.
"CityWalk has the potential to be positioned more deliberately – not as generic retail space, but as a curated commercial marketplace layer that translates i-City's AI ecosystem into everyday consumer engagement," he said.
According to Tan, such positioning could enable CityWalk to emerge as a distinct, AI- and robotics-themed retail environment, rather than a conventional retail space.
"If CityWalk is positioned well, it would not only strengthen its own appeal but also add value to the mall. A differentiated CityWalk can create fresh reasons for people to spend more time within i-City, and that can benefit all components within i-City," he said.
Tan noted that AIWEC plays an important role as a real, experiential touchpoint for AI and robotics within the township. It introduces visitors to these technologies, and CityWalk can extend that experience into retail, services and recurring engagement.
"These components help shape how people experience the township. Retail can then build on that by translating those ideas into more regular, everyday engagement. In that sense, CityWalk can do more than function as an extension of retail space. It can serve as the commercial marketplace layer of i-City's broader AI ecosystem, connecting experience, lifestyle and commerce, emerging as Malaysia's first AI and robotics-themed retail environment."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Friends reunite every Ramadan to sell jar-roasted chickens
Article:
KOTA BARU: For the past 14 years, three friends have drawn customers to their stall at Pintu Geng here with their ayam tempayan (chicken roasted inside large earthen jars).
Using a traditional method that relies on heat and steam trapped inside the jars, the trio sold more than 100 chickens a day during Ramadan.
Mohd Deraman Husin, 51, who works at the canteen of Sekolah Rendah Sri Kota, said the business began as a small venture among friends.
"We only sell ayam tempayan once a year, but the response has always been very encouraging," he said when met.
The father of four said the chickens were marinated with their own homemade spice blend before being cooked for about an hour in five jars.
"The difference with cooking in a tempayan is the steam trapped inside. It keeps the meat juicy while the skin becomes nicely roasted," he added.
His friend Hassan Shaari, 45, a chef from Kuala Krai, said the tempayan used to roast the chickens were specially ordered to ensure the right heat and cooking method.
"Each chicken was sold according to its size, usually between RM23 and RM26," he said.
Their friend, Mohd Zahudi Hassan, 60, a motorcycle mechanic, said the business was as much about friendship as it was about food.
"We started this together many years ago, and we still enjoy doing it. Customers keep coming back because they like the taste and the traditional way we cook the chicken," he said.
Despite only operating occasionally, the trio hoped to continue the tradition for years to come, keeping their friendship — and their tempayan fires — burning strong.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Alif Ahmad ordered to pay RM130,000 over defamatory posts
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Shah Alam High Court today ordered social media activist Ahmad Aliff A S Ahmad Shariffuddin, better known as Alif Ahmad, to pay RM130,000 in damages to two lawyers for publishing defamatory statements on social media.
The ruling ended a legal dispute that began in late 2021, when the plaintiffs filed a civil suit to restore their professional reputations.
Judge Datuk Khadijah Idris ruled that the first plaintiff, Nur A'minahtul Mardiah Md Nor, had proven defamation in relation to the second publication (F2).
The court awarded her RM80,000 in global damages and RM10,000 in costs.
The second plaintiff, Asiah Abd Jalil, also succeeded in her claim involving the sixth publication (F6), with the court awarding RM50,000 in damages and RM7,000 in legal costs.
Ahmad Aliff's counterclaim against both lawyers was dismissed.
In the writ of summons, filed through lawyer Hafizah Hassan of Messrs Nurul Hafidzah & Associates, the plaintiffs had sought RM1 million each in general damages and a public apology on Facebook.
They said the statements caused serious reputational damage and disrupted their professional duties.
The writ stated that failure to comply with the judgment could result in committal proceedings for contempt of court.
Lawyer Mahmud Jumaat, representing Ahmad Aliff, said an appeal would be filed soon.
He added that only two out of 19 claims were allowed by the court.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Swatch CEO concerned about surge in value of Swiss franc
Article:
ZURICH: Swatch Group chief executive officer Nick Hayek has expressed concern about the strength of the Swiss franc, Swiss broadcaster SRF reported, saying the safe-haven currency's appreciation was putting domestic industry at risk.
The franc has recently risen to 11-year highs against the euro, and has also continued to appreciate against the dollar, making Swiss products more expensive abroad and squeezing manufacturers' profit margins.
Hayek, whose company owns watch brands including Longines, Omega and Tissot, accused the Swiss National Bank of not responding to the appreciation for fear of being branded a currency manipulator by Washington.
"We have nothing against a strong Swiss franc," Hayek told SRF. "But its current strength against all currencies is so excessive that it's becoming impossible to continue manufacturing industrial products in this country.
"My concern is that the Swiss National Bank isn't recognising the problem," he added. "It's remaining silent, fearing that US President Donald Trump might see it as a currency manipulator and punish it with higher tariffs."
The SNB, which is due to announce its latest monetary policy decision later on Thursday, declined to comment. Earlier this the central month said it was more willing to intervene in foreign currency markets to check a rapid and excessive appreciation of the franc.
Hayek warned that smaller manufacturers could be forced to move production abroad or shut down.
"When industry struggles, the whole country suffers. It's about the social fabric: apprentices, factories, the know-how to manufacture a product in Switzerland. I don't want to do without that," he said.
Swatch Group was also seeking reimbursement for US customs duties paid under tariffs imposed by Trump, with potential refunds amounting to tens of millions, Hayek said.
"Everyone wants to get back unjustly paid customs duties. Our branches in America are already working on it, together with our lawyers," Hayek said.
"In the end, we're talking about refunds in the tens of millions, and the chances are good that we'll get it back."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Batik Air starts daily Penang–Johor Bahru flights
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Batik Air has launched a daily direct service between Penang and Johor Bahru effective today, in a bid to strengthen connectivity between the northern and southern regions.
The move follows strong demand during the recent Chinese New Year travel period, when additional flights were temporarily deployed to accommodate a surge in passengers returning home.
The airline said the encouraging response from customers highlighted the growing need for more direct domestic routes that bypass Kuala Lumpur.
Penang International Airport recorded 741,183 passenger movements in January, making it one of Malaysia's busiest aviation hubs after Kuala Lumpur International Airport. Johor's Senai International Airport is also gaining traction as a key gateway to the south.
Batik Air chief executive officer Datuk Chandran Rama Muthy said the new service reflects the airline's commitment to strengthening Malaysia's domestic air network in response to evolving travel patterns.
"These two cities play important roles in Malaysia's tourism, trade and business landscape, and this service brings them closer together while making travel more seamless for passengers," he said in a statement.
Looking ahead, the airline said enhancing domestic connectivity remains a key priority in supporting the country's Visit Malaysia 2026-driven tourism ambitions, as well as economic growth.
Chandran said Batik Air will continue exploring opportunities to expand routes that improve accessibility between key regions nationwide, with the Penang-Johor Bahru service marking another step towards a more connected domestic network.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Chow rebuts claims of being out of touch with the community
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow has dismissed claims that he was out of touch with grassroots concerns, stressing that his frequent statewide engagements, including the ongoing Jelajah Ramadan programme, demonstrated otherwise.
Responding to criticism that he should personally verify issues, such as the recent controversy over quit rent hikes instead of relying on official briefings, Chow said such assertions were inaccurate.
"Don't worry, I am always on the ground.
"If you follow my Facebook, you will see I go here and there regularly," he told reporters at his office in Komtar today.
Bagan member of parliament Lim Guan Eng had called on Chow to go to the ground to better understand the people's plight over sharply increased quit rent rates, instead of relying solely on feedback from officials.
Lim said Chow's own admission that the new quit rent calculation system had resulted in increases of hundreds and even thousands of times
underscored
the severity of the issue, particularly as such hikes were not outlined in Pakatan Harapan's manifesto for the 2023 state election.
Elaborating, Chow said the ongoing Jelajah Ramadan had taken him to all five districts in Penang, enabling direct engagement with residents and local communities.
"Surprisingly, during my Jelajah throughout Ramadan, the quit rent issue was not raised directly to me. Not that it does not exist, but perhaps it was not highlighted during those sessions," he added.
Chow said such outreach efforts were not seasonal, noting that his office organised Jelajah programmes throughout the year.
"We will continue. In fact, my official team arranges Jelajah every year. I think no other leaders can outperform me in terms of visiting kawasan across the state," he said.
On claims that the state government's quit rent data was unreliable, Chow described them as misinterpretations.
"That is their interpretation, which is totally wrong. Where mistakes occur, they will be rectified, and some have already been corrected," he said.
He said more than 2,000 quit rent-related cases had been received, with over 500 already decided, while the rest were undergoing verification before decisions were made.
Chow reiterated that the calculation of quit rent was based on land size, usage and applicable rates, describing it as a straightforward process.
He said higher assessments in certain cases involved land still under master titles but already being used for industrial or commercial activities.
"It involves ongoing developments that have not reached the subdivision stage, so the rate is imposed based on the mother lot," he said.
Chow acknowledged that delays in subdivision could be linked to planning processes, adding that such matters were being addressed.
On enforcement against illegal land use, he said local authorities preferred a "pemutihan", or regularisation approach, over punitive action.
"We want them to be legalised, operate without fear, and comply with the rules. Enforcement is an option, but not the only option," he said.
He said that enforcement would be taken when necessary, particularly in response to public complaints or continued non-compliance.
Chow also advised business operators against illegally converting residential properties for commercial use, urging them instead to operate from approved premises.
Separately, he addressed criticism that the state government's initiatives were not part of its election manifesto, saying such arguments were misplaced.
"The manifesto outlines what a party commits to when contesting elections, and we report progress on those commitments annually in the state assembly," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hong Kong national among four held in Bukit Damansara gambling raid
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Four people, including a Hong Kong national, were arrested after police busted a syndicate organising "mini casino" gambling under the guise of a sports event organising company.
The raid at 3.30pm yesterday, at an office premises along Jalan Gelenggang in Bukit Damansara, was conducted by the City police D7 (Secret Societies, Gambling, and Vice) Division.
City police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said those arrested were a Hong Kong male national and three others aged between 27 and 49.
He said investigations revealed that the syndicate was involved in managing, organising and promoting "mini casino" gambling.
"Police detected that the group used several websites and social media platforms to promote the mini casino operations.
"The syndicate targeted Asian countries, namely Taiwan, Korea, the Philippines and Thailand, as locations for their events.
"Investigations found that the group has been in operation since 2022," he said in a statement.
He added that investigations were ongoing to determine the full modus operandi of the syndicate.
"The case is being investigated under Section 4(1)(g) of the Common Gaming Houses Act 1953 and Section 6(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1969/63," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Foreign investors dump Japanese stocks on Iran war jitters
Article:
TOKYO: Foreign investors were heavy sellers of Japanese stocks in the week through March 14, driven by worries about the economic fallout from a prolonged Iran war and sharply higher energy prices.
Foreigners sold about 1.77 trillion yen (US$11.09 billion) worth of Japanese stocks in their biggest weekly net sales since Sept 13, 2025, snapping an 11-week buying streak, data from Japan's Finance Ministry showed.
The Nikkei sank nearly three per cent on Thursday as Iran, in a major escalation in its war with the US and Israel, attacked several energy facilities across the Middle East, following a strike on its South Pars gas field on Wednesday.
The Bank of Japan, keeping interest rates unchanged earlier in the day, warned of the impact rising oil costs from the Middle East conflict could have on underlying inflation.
Overseas investors bought a net 1.19 trillion yen of Japanese long-term bonds last week, reversing the prior week's net sales of 963.6 billion yen.
They remained net sellers of short-term Japanese bonds for a second consecutive week, offloading 952.6 billion yen.
At the same time, Japanese investors bought a net 950.7 billion yen of foreign equities, their biggest net investment for a week since early May 2025.
However, they sold about 992 billion yen worth of foreign long-term bonds and 262.9 billion yen worth of short-term instruments, marking their fourth weekly net sales in the last five weeks. (US$1 = 159.64 yen)
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BOJ leaves benchmark interest rate unchanged amid Iran war
Article:
TOKYO: The Bank of Japan kept its benchmark interest rate unchanged at around 0.75 per cent at the end of its two-day policy meeting on Thursday as it gauges the impact of the Iran conflict and surging crude oil prices on the Japanese economy, Kyodo News Agency reported.
The bank held the key rate steady for the second straight meeting after raising it to its highest level in 30 years at its December meeting.
With uncertainties heightening over the effects of the West Asia crisis on inflation and the broader economy, the central bank is in a difficult position as it seeks to continue to raise interest rates to stably achieve its two per cent inflation target.
The US-Israeli war against Iran, launched on Feb 28, has led to the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, through which many oil tankers pass, including those heading to Japan. There has also been damage to facilities exporting liquefied natural gas.
Soaring crude oil prices will likely push up the prices of a wide range of products from gasoline to plastics, while electricity and gas bills could also rise if liquefied natural gas prices climb in tandem with those of oil.
Hiking interest rates is generally seen as effective in taming inflation, but higher borrowing costs could raise the risks of stagflation -- a state of high inflation and sluggish economic growth.
After the BOJ hiked the policy rate in December, the central bank kept it steady the following month, citing the need to assess the impact of the hike on businesses and households.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police foil petrol smuggling bid at Bukit Kayu Hitam, five arrested
Article:
JITRA: Police have foiled an attempt to smuggle about 2,000 litres of petrol after intercepting five pickup trucks at the Bukit Kayu Hitam Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex.
Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said the operation, carried out by a team from the Kubang Pasu district police headquarters, took place within an hour from 7am yesterday.
Five men — four Thai nationals and a local aged between 23 and 37 — were arrested.
"Initial investigations found that the suspects used pickup trucks modified with additional steel tanks.
"The installation of the tanks was intended to illegally smuggle petrol to neighbouring countries," he said at a press conference at the Kubang Pasu district police headquarters.
Also present was Kedah Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) director Muhammad Nizam Jamaludin.
Adzli said the suspects, along with the seized items, had been handed over to KPDN for further investigation under Section 21 of the Control of Supplies Act 1961 and Regulation 3(1) of the Control of Supplies Regulations 1974.
He added that none of the suspects had prior criminal records and all tested negative for drugs. The total value of the seizure, including the vehicles, was estimated at RM560,000.
"The pickup trucks use diesel, so why were they carrying petrol? This is a serious offence," he said, adding that rising fuel prices in neighbouring countries had contributed to smuggling activities.
Police are also investigating the petrol stations believed to have supplied fuel to the suspects.
"I urge oil operators in the state to act responsibly and not be complicit in smuggling activities that can affect the country's economy and threaten public safety.
"We will continue to intensify operations and monitoring, especially along border areas, to curb the smuggling of controlled and subsidised goods," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Steel Hawk secures three-year maintenance contract in Pengerang
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Steel Hawk Bhd has secured a term contract for general maintenance of workshop machining at the Centralised Maintenance Workshop in the Pengerang Integrated Complex in Johor.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company said the three-year contract was awarded to its unit, Steel Hawk Engineering Sdn Bhd, by PRPC Utilities & Facilities Sdn Bhd.
"The contract is on a call-out basis and does not have a fixed value. Steel Hawk will provide specified services as and when required throughout the contract period," it said.
Steel Hawk said it expects the contract to contribute positively to its earnings for the financial year ending Dec 31, 2026.
At the noon break, Steel Hawk's shares were unchanged at 29 sen, with 74,000 shares traded, giving it a market capitalisation of RM142.26 million.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Stocks slump on worsening war in Middle East, frail yen in focus
Article:
SINGAPORE: Stocks slid and oil prices rose sharply on Thursday after a major escalation in the US and Israel's war with Iran rattled investors, while the Bank of Japan became the latest central bank to warn about the impact of energy costs on inflation.
The yen wobbled near the psychologically significant 160 per dollar level as the BOJ left interest rates unchanged, with traders looking for signs of intervention after strong comments from Japanese finance minister Satsuki Katayama earlier in the day.
Focus remains on the war in the Middle East as investors come to the realisation that the conflict is shaping up to be a prolonged one, stoking the risk of stagflation – weak growth coupled with high inflation.
US President Donald Trump said an angry Israel "violently lashed out" and attacked Iran's major gas field, prompting a threat by Iran to attack oil and gas targets across the Gulf, while it fired missiles at Qatar and Saudi Arabia.
The hits to energy infrastructure sent shockwaves across markets as US crude futures extended gains above US$97 per barrel. Natural gas surged three per cent, while Brent futures rose to US$111.87 a barrel, up four per cent on the day.
In stocks, Japan's Nikkei was down over three per cent, while South Korean equities fell 2.8 per cent. MSCI's broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan fell more than 2.5 per cent. European futures were down more than 1.5 per cent.
"This latest escalation feels like a turning point for markets because the conflict is no longer just about military headlines or Strait of Hormuz closure," said Charu Chanana, chief investment strategist at Saxo in Singapore.
"It is now hitting the plumbing of the global energy system. What is unsettling markets now is the growing stagflation risk... It means this is no longer just a geopolitical story but a macro one."
The dollar strengthened across the board, also buoyed by the Federal Reserve predicting just one more cut this year as the central bank left rates unchanged on Wednesday. Traders, though, are no longer fully pricing in any easing in 2026.
The dollar index, which measures the US currency against six other units, is up 2.5 per cent since the war broke out at the end of February as investors look to the greenback as the haven of choice. The index was last at 100.15, slightly lower after a 0.7 per cent rise on Wednesday.
The two-year US Treasury yield, which typically reflects near-term rate expectations, was up roughly six basis points in Asia to 3.8051 per cent, its highest since August 2025.
ECB AND BOE UP NEXT
In a week filled with policy meetings across the globe, investors have been parsing through comments to gauge the impact of the war, with the European Central Bank and Bank of England due later in the day.
As widely expected, the Bank of Japan left unchanged its short-term policy rate at 0.75 per cent but joined the US Federal Reserve and Bank of Canada in striking a cautious tone about the war and pricing pressures.
BOJ governor Kazuo Ueda, at his post-meeting briefing, will aim to frame the balance between the need to support a shock-hit economy and avoid being behind the curve on inflation in the face of a persistently weak yen.
The yen was last at 159.71 per dollar, having dropped over two per cent against the dollar this month.
Fred Neumann, chief Asia economist at HSBC, said the path ahead for the BOJ is narrowing, noting rising price pressures, from soaring energy costs and a weaker currency, are pointing to prompt and decisive tightening.
"Governor Ueda will likely want to keep his options open for the coming months. As elsewhere, monetary officials will want to play for time, seeing out the conflict in the Middle East evolves in the coming weeks and its impact on global energy and financial markets."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang establishes PIFC task force to position state as tech‑finance hub, white paper due 3Q, says CM
Article:
GEORGE TOWN, March 19 — Penang will establish a Special Task Force Committee to continue efforts to set up the Penang International Financial Centre (PIFC), based on a “whole-of-state” approach.
Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the decision, made during the state executive council meeting yesterday, was a step to strengthen the state’s commitment to realising the initiative.
“I will chair the committee, while the State Secretary (Datuk Seri Zulkifli Long) will serve as deputy chairman, with members including the state financial officer, state legal adviser, representatives from the state economic planning unit (BPEN), Invest Penang, the Northern Corridor Implementation Authority (NCIA), the Malaysian Investment Development Authority (MIDA), and representatives from the private sector.
“The committee will be jointly managed by the state finance department and Penang Institute, which had previously assisted the state government in exploring the establishment of the PIFC, including conducting engagement sessions with various stakeholders and regulators,” he told a press conference in Komtar today.
At the initial stage, he said the special task force will be responsible for preparing a White Paper and a strategic action plan (blueprint) to be submitted to the Ministry of Finance Malaysia (MOF) by the third quarter of this year.
Elaborating further, Chow said the establishment of the PIFC aims, among others, to position Penang as a technology and financial nexus hub, particularly in supporting the semiconductor industry value chain, including the development of strategic financial services such as supply chain financing, Islamic finance-based fintech, and digital financial infrastructure.
He added that it is also in line with efforts to strengthen Penang’s position as a major electrical and electronics (E&E) hub through capital inflows resulting from global trade conflicts, thereby positioning the state as a low-risk investment destination.
He said the development of the PIFC is expected to take up to 20 years, but the initial implementation can begin in the near term by focusing on institutional establishment and regulatory ecosystem development.
Chow also informed that the first meeting of the special task force is scheduled for April 1 to begin detailed discussions toward preparing the blueprint and white paper.
Commenting on the location of the PIFC, he said the matter has yet to be finalised and is subject to study outcomes and approval by the federal government. So far, three companies based on the island have submitted proposals through an expression of interest process conducted by Penang Institute.
In March last year, the Penang Chief Minister’s Office officially submitted a letter of intent to the MOF regarding the proposal to establish an international financial centre in the state. However, the MOF gave a neutral response in July 2025, stating that there is currently no national policy to establish a new international financial centre.
Nevertheless, Chow said recent developments indicate a more positive response, with the federal government requesting the state provide a clear policy framework and conduct in-depth studies and comprehensive stakeholder engagement. — Bernama

Title: Couple killed in crash after car veers into opposite lane in Kota Tinggi, four others injured
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, March 19 — A couple were killed in an accident involving two vehicles at kilometre 77.3 of the Johor Bahru–Mersing road near Kota Tinggi this morning.
Kota Tinggi district police chief Supt Yusof Othman said the victims were travelling in a Nissan Sentra, which collided with a Proton Saga carrying four people in the incident at about 9.45 am.
He said the victims were a 26-year-old man who was driving the vehicle and a 25-year-old woman passenger.
“The victims, who were travelling from Mersing towards Johor Bahru, are believed to have entered the opposite lane and collided with the Proton Saga, which was travelling from Pasir Gudang to Kuantan,” he said in a statement.
He said both victims were pronounced dead at the scene due to severe head and body injuries and were sent to the Forensic Unit of Kota Tinggi Hospital.
However, he said a 23-year-old woman who was driving the Proton Saga, along with three male passengers aged between 16 and 21, who are siblings, sustained only minor injuries. — Bernama

Title: Kota Kinabalu police warn of vehicle scam tactics as online fraud losses hit RM11.12m
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, March 19 — The Kota Kinabalu District Police Headquarters recorded losses totaling RM11.12 million from 214 online fraud cases between January and March 15.
Acting Kota Kinabalu police chief Supt Syed Lot Syed Ab Rahman said these cases form part of the 290 commercial crime investigations opened by the Commercial Crime Investigation Division, with total losses amounting to RM14.628 million.
He added that, besides online scams, 46 cases involved e-commerce fraud, particularly vehicle transactions, resulting in losses of RM328,888.
Commenting on the vehicle fraud tactics, he said syndicates acted cunningly as intermediaries by reposting images of vehicles owned by others on social media platforms such as Facebook and Mudah.my at significantly lower prices.
“When victims show interest, the syndicates negotiate the price and direct them to the original owner’s address to view the vehicle. At the same time, they inform the real vehicle owner that a representative (the victim) will be coming to inspect the car.
“Once the victim is satisfied and agrees to buy, the syndicates request payment before supposedly handing over the vehicle,” he said in a statement today.
Syed Lot said that after victims transferred payment to the syndicate’s account, the actual vehicle owners refused to hand over the cars as they had not received any money, while the syndicates would disappear and block all contact with the victims.
He said investigations found that the e-commerce syndicates merely acted as third-party intermediaries online and never met either the victims or the genuine vehicle owners.
Meanwhile, he also advised the public to remain vigilant against scam syndicates offering fake homestay bookings through social media.
He said such scams often target urban and tourist areas, especially during school holidays and festive seasons, and urged the public to conduct thorough checks before making any payments.
“Police advise the public not to be easily swayed by advertisements on social media or offers that seem too cheap, and to verify the identity of vehicle owners or service providers before carrying out any transactions,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Evening commute alert: Thunderstorms to hit KL, Putrajaya, Selangor, multiple other states
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued an urgent weather advisory for the Klang Valley and several other states, warning of heavy rain, thunderstorms, and strong winds expected to persist until 6pm today.
According to the alert issued at 3.50 pm, the affected areas in Peninsular Malaysia include the entire Federal Territories of Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya, as well as the Selangor districts of Hulu Selangor, Gombak, Petaling, Hulu Langat, and Sepang.
In the northern and central regions, residents in Kulim and Bandar Baharu in Kedah should prepare for adverse weather, alongside a significant portion of Perak, specifically Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang, and Muallim.
The warning also extends to the Pahang districts of Cameron Highlands, Lipis, and Raub.
For East Malaysia, the weather alert covers a wide expanse of Sarawak, including Sri Aman, Betong (Pusa, Saratok, and Kabong), Sibu (Selangau), Mukah, Bintulu, Miri (Subis, Beluru, Telang Usan, and Marudi), and Limbang.
In Sabah, thunderstorms are forecast to impact the Interior districts of Sipitang, Tenom, and Nabawan, as well as the Tawau region.
MetMalaysia issues these short-term warnings when there are signs of imminent thunderstorms with a rainfall intensity exceeding 20 mm/hour or when such conditions are expected to last for more than an hour.
These alerts remain valid for a maximum of six hours from the time of issuance.
As the warning period overlaps with the peak evening commute, motorists and residents in the highlighted areas are advised to remain vigilant against potential flash floods and the risk of falling trees due to sudden strong winds.

Title: Madani govt ensuring sufficient supply of basic items, public urged not to panic buy, says Mustapha Sakmud
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, March 19 — The Madani Government will continue striving to ensure that the basic needs of the people are sufficient, especially in Sabah and Sarawak, in the face of current economic challenges, said Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Sabah and Sarawak) Datuk Seri Mustapha Sakmud.
He said the measures included maintaining fuel prices and ensuring the supply of basic goods remained stable so that the people would not be burdened by the rising cost of living.
“So, we hope it will get better, and we will not feel the effects of the (West Asia) war crisis. But at the same time, the Madani Government will try to ensure our basic needs are sufficient.
“And we are also maintaining prices, particularly petrol and diesel. Especially in Sabah, we are ensuring that the people are not burdened by the sudden increase in costs right now,” he said here today.
He said this after attending “Raya Shirt Purchase Donation Programme for Asnaf Students’ organised by the Sepanggar Member of Parliament’s Office at a clothing premises in Alam Mesra here.
Meanwhile, Mustapha, who is also the Sepanggar Member of Parliament, advised the public not to engage in panic buying, as the supply of basic items, including rice, is still adequate.
“In terms of supply, we are actually sufficient. So, we hope the public won’t panic… the situation is still under control. So, we must plan, and what is important is that we spend wisely. That’s the only approach now… be thrifty,” he said.
He said that 50 pupils from Sekolah Kebangsaan (SK) Gantisan and SK Pengiran Siti Hafsah in Karambunai were selected to participate in the programme this year.
He added that schools are selected on a rotation basis for the annual programme, which is entering its third year.
According to him, the selection of participants is made by school authorities, with priority given to those in the B40 group, including those listed in the e-Kasih system, orphans and students from underprivileged families. — Bernama

Title: IGP defends handcuffing Zamri Vinoth, says procedure follows Sedition Act requirements
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — Inspector-General of Police (IGP) Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said Zamri Vinoth was brought to court in handcuffs on Tuesday in accordance with legal procedures and regulations.
He said Zamri’s initial arrest involved a case under Section 505 of the Penal Code, and he is also being investigated under the Sedition Act 1948, which requires a different procedural approach.
“The offences for which we made previous arrests vary. Some are bailable and some are not, particularly during the early stage of the arrests involving four cases in Putrajaya under Section 505 of the Penal Code.
“At the same time, there are cases under the Sedition Act that require their own specific procedures,” he said at a press conference today.
Mohd Khalid added that these differing procedures may confuse the public, but he assured that all processes are carried out in accordance with the law and represent standard practice within the legal system.
On Tuesday, Zamri and activist Arun Dorasamy were among three individuals brought to court in different states over charges of making statements that could cause public alarm.
Zamri, 42, whose full name is Muhammad Zamri Vinoth Kalimuthu, was charged at the Kuala Lumpur Sessions Court; Arun, 56, whose full name is Arumugam Dorasamy, was charged at the Jawi Magistrate’s Court; while e-hailing driver P. Mahendra Bhoopaty, 53, faced similar charges at the Seremban Magistrate’s Court.
Zamri and Arun pleaded not guilty, while Mahendra Bhoopaty pleaded guilty and was fined RM4,000 in default seven months in jail.
Commenting on activist Tamim Dahri’s whereabouts, Mohd Khalid said authorities have information that he is currently in the holy land, and legal proceedings against him have already been initiated.
“When he returns, we will take action against him,” he said.
On Monday, Mohd Khalid had informed that Tamim would be charged at the Langkawi Magistrate’s Court in Kedah under Section 295 of the Penal Code for allegedly damaging the soolam, a sacred Hindu symbol, at the former site of the Sri Maha Muniswarar Temple in Langkawi, Kedah. — Bernama

Title: IGP: Businessman Victor Chin linked to money laundering ‘matters’, currently abroad
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — Police have linked businessman Victor Chin, a central figure in the ongoing “corporate mafia” probe, to activities under the Anti-Money Laundering Act (AMLA), Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail  said today.
The IGP also disclosed that Chin, who has been openly critical of the investigation against him, is currently abroad as authorities seek international cooperation to advance their investigation,
The Star
reported.
“We have identified several matters that require action under AMLA,” Mohd Khalid said at a press conference at Bukit Aman on Thursday.
“As everyone is aware, he is currently overseas, and there are ongoing efforts to secure cooperation from relevant international parties for the investigation.”
The confirmation follows a high-profile raid on March 16, where police and officers from the Securities Commission (SC) searched several premises belonging to Chin in Kemensah and Sungai Long, Kajang.
The IGP clarified that the raid was conducted as part of the SC’s own probe under the same anti-money laundering laws.
The multi-agency investigation into claims of corporate manipulation was initiated following a directive from the Cabinet, involving PDRM, the SC, the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC), and the Inland Revenue Board (LHDN).
In a previous statement, Chin, who is the executive director of MMAG Holdings Bhd, denied any involvement in forcing company takeovers.
He acknowledged that such “mafia tactics” exist but insisted he was a victim, not an orchestrator.
Today, Chin also accused the police of “aerial bombardment” tactics in their investigation against him, claiming they were going after any company or individual even tangentially linked to him.
Separately, the IGP also commented on the police investigation into the international news agency Bloomberg, stating that several persons of interest in that case are also overseas.
He confirmed that police had already interviewed several Malaysia-based Bloomberg staff at their Kuala Lumpur office on February 26.

Title: Cabinet ministers extend Ugadi greetings, call for unity and harmony
Article:
